revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 (西の谷) |所在地=愛媛県新居浜市磯浦町 |祭神=[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] }} '''大山祇神社 (西の谷)'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町にある[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[新居浜市]]の西端である磯浦町・西の谷の高台に位置する。
創建やご祭神など詳細な点は不明だが、同じ愛媛県内の大三島にある[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えているとされる。
1585年の[[天正の陣]]で兵火により焼失。延宝2(1674)年に再建されたと伝えられている。
== 歴史 == かつて西の谷の北側は[[瀬戸内海]]に面していたが、現在は[[住友グループ]]の工業地域（埋立地）となっている。
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]]

revision.comment: [[Category:伊予国の式内社]]を除去 ([[WP:HotCat|HotCat]]使用)

== 歴史 == かつて西の谷の北側は[[瀬戸内海]]に面していたが、現在は[[住友グループ]]の工業地域（埋立地）となっている。

== 歴史 == [[新居浜市]]の西端である磯浦町・西の谷の高台に位置する。
創建やご祭神など詳細な点は不明だが、同じ愛媛県内の大三島にある[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えているとされる。
1585年の[[天正の陣]]で兵火により焼失。延宝2(1674)年に再建されたと伝えられている。
== 歴史 == かつて西の谷の北側は[[瀬戸内海]]に面していたが、現在は[[住友グループ]]の工業地域（埋立地）となっている。
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]]

{{特筆性|date=2019-12}} {{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 (西の谷) |所在地=愛媛県新居浜市磯浦町 |祭神=[[オオヤマツミ|大山積神]] }} '''大山祇神社 (西の谷)'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町にある[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]の瀬戸内側の西端である磯浦町・西の谷の高台に位置する。
創建やご祭神など詳細な点は不明だが、同じ愛媛県内の大三島町宮浦にある山祇神社（大山祇神社）の総本社[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えているとされる。
毛利軍と小早川軍が侵攻した[[天正]]13年（1585年）の[[羽柴秀吉]]による四国攻め（[[天正の陣]]）の際、その兵火により焼失。[[延宝]]2年(1674)年に再建されたと伝えられている。
== 地域 == [[西条市]]と隣接する[[新居浜市]]の北西部の山間に位置する。かつて磯浦西の谷の北側は[[瀬戸内海]]に面していたが、現在は[[住友グループ]]の工業地域（埋立地）となっている。
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]]

毛利軍と小早川軍が侵攻した[[天正]]13年（1585年）の[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による四国攻め（[[天正の陣]]）の際、その兵火により焼失。[[延宝]]2年(1674)年に再建されたと伝えられている。
== 地域 == [[西条市]]と隣接する[[新居浜市]]の北西部の山間に位置する。かつて磯浦西の谷の北側は[[瀬戸内海]]に面していたが、現在は[[住友グループ]]の工業地域（埋立地）となっている。
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]]

== 地域 == [[西条市]]と隣接する[[新居浜市]]の北西端エリアに位置する。南側には小高い山があり神社周辺も高台となっている。かつて磯浦西の谷の北側直ぐは[[瀬戸内海]]に面していたが、現在は[[住友グループ]]の埋立地域となっており、この一帯には工場などが広がっている。

{{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 (西の谷) |画像= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|境内]] |所在地=[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町18 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 53.429 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 133 |経度分 = 14 | 経度秒 = 44.176 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''大山祇神社 (西の谷)'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町にある[[神社]]。瀬戸内側の西端である磯浦町・西の谷の高台に位置する。
== 概要 == [[西条市]]と隣接する[[新居浜市]]の北西端エリアの西の谷地区の自治会館のすぐ南に位置する。北側に突き出た尾根の中腹にあり、入口には石の鳥居があり高さ約20ｍの石段を上がるとは正面にプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られ、左側には石の祠があり、右上にもプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られている。
かつて磯浦西の谷の北側直ぐは[[瀬戸内海]]に面していて潮干狩りや海水浴などもおこなわれていた。現在は[[住友グループ]]の埋立地域となっており、この一帯には工場などが広がっている。
== 歴史 == 創建などの詳細な点は不明だが、古くからこの地に祀られていたとされ、ご祭神は同じ愛媛県内の大三島町宮浦にある山祇神社（大山祇神社）の総本社[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えている。
毛利軍と小早川軍が侵攻した[[天正]]13年（1585年）の[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による四国攻め（[[天正の陣]]）の際、その兵火により焼失。[[延宝]]2年(1674)年に再建されたと伝えられている。
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 (西の谷) |画像= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|境内]] |所在地=[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町18 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 53.429 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 133 |経度分 = 14 | 経度秒 = 44.176 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''大山祇神社 (西の谷)'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町にある[[神社]]。瀬戸内側の西端である磯浦町・西の谷の高台に位置する。

かつて磯浦西の谷の北側直ぐは[[瀬戸内海]]に面していて潮干狩りや海水浴などもおこなわれていた。現在は[[住友グループ]]の埋立地域となっており、この一帯には工場などが広がっている。
== 歴史 == 創建などの詳細な点は不明だが、古くからこの地に祀られていたとされ、ご祭神は同じ愛媛県内の大三島町宮浦にある山祇神社（大山祇神社）の総本社[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えている。
毛利軍と小早川軍が侵攻した[[天正]]13年（1585年）の[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による四国攻め（[[天正の陣]]）の際、その兵火により焼失。[[延宝]]2年(1674)年に再建された。
[[新居郡|神野郡]]の興隆に尽力した[[寂仙|上仙法師]]とゆかりがある神社の一つとの伝承が残されている。
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]]

== 概要 == [[西条市]]と隣接する[[新居浜市]]の北西端エリアの西の谷地区の自治会館のすぐ南に位置する。北側に突き出た尾根の中腹にあり、入口には石の鳥居があり高さ約20ｍの石段を上がるとは正面にプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られ、左側には石の祠があり、右上にもプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られている。
かつて磯浦西の谷の北側直ぐは[[瀬戸内海]]に面していて潮干狩りや海水浴などもおこなわれていた。現在は[[住友グループ]]の埋立地域となっており、この一帯には工場などが広がっている。
== 歴史 == 創建などの詳細な点は不明だが、古くからこの地に祀られていたとされ、ご祭神は同じ愛媛県内の大三島町宮浦にある山祇神社（大山祇神社）の総本社[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えている。
毛利軍と小早川軍が侵攻した[[天正]]13年（1585年）の[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による四国攻め（[[天正の陣]]）の際、その兵火により焼失。[[延宝]]2年(1674)年に再建された。
[[新居郡|神野郡]]の興隆に尽力した[[寂仙|上仙法師]]とゆかりがある神社の一つとの伝承が残されている。
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2024-02 | 特筆性 = 2024-02 }}{{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 (西の谷) |画像= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|境内]] |所在地=[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町18 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 53.429 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 133 |経度分 = 14 | 経度秒 = 44.176 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''大山祇神社 (西の谷)'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町にある[[神社]]。瀬戸内側の西端である磯浦町・西の谷の高台に位置する。
== 概要 == [[西条市]]と隣接する[[新居浜市]]の北西端エリアの西の谷地区の自治会館のすぐ南に位置する。北側に突き出た尾根の中腹にあり、入口には石の鳥居があり高さ約20ｍの石段を上がるとは正面にプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られ、左側には石の祠があり、右上にもプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られている。
かつて磯浦西の谷の北側直ぐは[[瀬戸内海]]に面していて潮干狩りや海水浴などもおこなわれていた。現在は[[住友グループ]]の埋立地域となっており、この一帯には工場などが広がっている。
== 歴史 == 創建などの詳細な点は不明だが、古くからこの地に祀られていたとされ、ご祭神は同じ愛媛県内の大三島町宮浦にある山祇神社（大山祇神社）の総本社[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えている。
毛利軍と小早川軍が侵攻した[[天正]]13年（1585年）の[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による四国攻め（[[天正の陣]]）の際、その兵火により焼失。[[延宝]]2年(1674)年に再建された。
[[新居郡|神野郡]]の興隆に尽力した[[寂仙|上仙法師]]とゆかりがある神社の一つとの伝承が残されている。

{{Multiple issues | More citations needed = 2024-02 | Notability = 2024-02 }}{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani) |image= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|Sanctuary]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市}}, Isoura-chō 18 |coordinates= {{coord|33|56|53.429|N|133|14|44.176|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} |shrine_rank= |established= Unknown |main_hall= |alternate_names= |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== 概要 == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== 歴史 == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]]

{{Multiple issues | More citations needed = 2024-02 | Notability = 2024-02 }}{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani) |image= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|Sanctuary]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市}}, Isoura-chō 18 |coordinates= {{coord|33|56|53.429|N|133|14|44.176|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} |shrine_rank= |established= Unknown |main_hall= |alternate_names= |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== 概要 == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== 歴史 == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

{{Multiple issues | More citations needed = 2024-02 | Notability = 2024-02 }}{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani) |image= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|Sanctuary]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市}}, Isoura-chō 18 |coordinates= {{coord|33|56|53.429|N|133|14|44.176|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} |shrine_rank= |established= Unknown |main_hall= |alternate_names= |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== 概要 == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== 歴史 == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.
{{citation needed|おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]]

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== 概要 == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

{{Multiple issues | More citations needed = 2024-02 | Notability = 2024-02 }}{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani) |image= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|Sanctuary]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市}}, Isoura-chō 18 |coordinates= {{coord|33|56|53.429|N|133|14|44.176|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} |shrine_rank= |established= Unknown |main_hall= |alternate_names= |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷) up to rev 99255965
revision.text:
{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2024-02 | 特筆性 = 2024-02 }}{{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 (西の谷) |画像= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|境内]] |所在地=[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町18 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 53.429 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 133 |経度分 = 14 | 経度秒 = 44.176 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''大山祇神社 (西の谷)'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町にある[[神社]]。瀬戸内側の西端である磯浦町・西の谷の高台に位置する。
== 概要 == [[西条市]]と隣接する[[新居浜市]]の北西端エリアの西の谷地区の自治会館のすぐ南に位置する。北側に突き出た尾根の中腹にあり、入口には石の鳥居があり高さ約20ｍの石段を上がるとは正面にプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られ、左側には石の祠があり、右上にもプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られている。
かつて磯浦西の谷の北側直ぐは[[瀬戸内海]]に面していて潮干狩りや海水浴などもおこなわれていた。現在は[[住友グループ]]の埋立地域となっており、この一帯には工場などが広がっている。
== 歴史 == 創建などの詳細な点は不明だが、古くからこの地に祀られていたとされ、ご祭神は同じ愛媛県内の大三島町宮浦にある山祇神社（大山祇神社）の総本社[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えている。
毛利軍と小早川軍が侵攻した[[天正]]13年（1585年）の[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による四国攻め（[[天正の陣]]）の際、その兵火により焼失。[[延宝]]2年(1674)年に再建された。
[[新居郡|神野郡]]の興隆に尽力した[[寂仙|上仙法師]]とゆかりがある神社の一つとの伝承が残されている。
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2024-02 | 特筆性 = 2024-02 }}{{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 (西の谷) |画像= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|境内]] |所在地=[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町18 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 53.429 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 133 |経度分 = 14 | 経度秒 = 44.176 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''大山祇神社 (西の谷)'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]磯浦町にある[[神社]]。瀬戸内側の西端である磯浦町・西の谷の高台に位置する。
== 概要 == [[西条市]]と隣接する[[新居浜市]]の北西端エリアの西の谷地区の自治会館のすぐ南に位置する。北側に突き出た尾根の中腹にあり、入口には石の鳥居があり高さ約20ｍの石段を上がるとは正面にプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られ、左側には石の祠があり、右上にもプレハブがあり中に神棚が祀られている。
かつて磯浦西の谷の北側直ぐは[[瀬戸内海]]に面していて潮干狩りや海水浴などもおこなわれていた。現在は[[住友グループ]]の埋立地域となっており、この一帯には工場などが広がっている。
== 歴史 == 創建などの詳細な点は不明だが、古くからこの地に祀られていたとされ、ご祭神は同じ愛媛県内の大三島町宮浦にある山祇神社（大山祇神社）の総本社[[大山祇神社]]の全ての祭神を迎えている。
毛利軍と小早川軍が侵攻した[[天正]]13年（1585年）の[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による四国攻め（[[天正の陣]]）の際、その兵火により焼失。[[延宝]]2年(1674)年に再建された。
[[新居郡|神野郡]]の興隆に尽力した[[寂仙|上仙法師]]とゆかりがある神社の一つとの伝承が残されている。
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまづみじんじゃ}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:新居浜市の建築物]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

{{Multiple issues | More citations needed = 2024-02 | Notability = 2024-02 }}{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani) |image= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|Sanctuary]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Isoura-chō 18 |coordinates= {{coord|33|56|53.429|N|133|14|44.176|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= |established= Unknown |main_hall= |alternate_names= |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.
{{citation needed|おおやまづみじんじゃ}} == Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{translated page|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|version=99255965|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.
== Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.
== Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

{{Multiple issues | More citations needed = 2024-02 | Notability = 2024-02 }}{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani) |image= [[File:Ooyamazimi nisinotani 20211124 1.jpg|270px|Sanctuary]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Niihama|en|Niihama|WD=Q386628|lt=Niihama, Ehime}}, Isoura-chō 18 |coordinates= {{coord|33|56|53.429|N|133|14|44.176|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} |shrine_Rank= |established= Unknown |main_hall= |alternate_names= |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.
== Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)''' (大山祇神社 (西の谷)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in **Isoura-chō, Niihama, Ehime Prefecture**. It is situated on an elevated area in the **Nishi no Tani district**, at the **westernmost point** of **Isoura-chō** along the **Seto Inland Sea**.
== Overview == The shrine is located in the **northwesternmost area** of **Niihama City**, near its border with **Saijō City**, and sits just south of the **Nishi no Tani district community hall**. It is positioned on the middle of a ridge that extends northward. At the entrance, there is a **stone torii gate**, followed by a **20-meter stone staircase** leading up to a **prefabricated building** where a **kamidana (Shinto altar)** is enshrined. To the left, there is a **stone shrine**, and to the upper right, another **prefabricated building** housing a second kamidana.
Historically, the **northern part of Isoura Nishi no Tani** directly faced the **Seto Inland Sea**, where **clamming and swimming** were once common activities. Today, however, the area has been reclaimed by the **Sumitomo Group**, and **factories** now dominate the surrounding landscape.
== History == The exact **founding date** of the shrine is unknown, but it has long been worshiped in this location. The **enshrined deity** originates from the **main Ōyamazumi Shrine** in **Miyaura, Ōmishima Town, Ehime Prefecture**, which serves as the **head shrine of all Ōyamazumi Shrines**.
During the **Tenshō Campaign (1585 CE)**, when **Mōri and Kobayakawa forces** invaded under the command of **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, the shrine was **burned down** in the conflict. It was later **rebuilt in Enpō 2 (1674 CE)**.
The shrine is also said to be **associated with Jōsen-hōshi** (上仙法師), a Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the **development of Kamino District (神野郡)**.
== Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
